test_cases.description: Verify that the application fetches all user records successfully when the JSONPlaceholder API is available and returns a complete response.
test_cases.name: Successful fetch of all users from JSONPlaceholder

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is accessible and returns a valid list of user objects at /users.
test_setup.then: Logging shows start of fetch, successful retrieval, correct count of users, and logs of each user summary. No errors are reported.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Verify that the application properly reports a failure and does not proceed when the JSONPlaceholder API is unreachable.
test_cases.name: API unreachable scenario

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is down or network is unavailable.
test_setup.then: An error is logged indicating the API is unreachable, and no further processing or user summary logging occurs.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Verify application behavior when the JSONPlaceholder API returns an empty user list.
test_cases.name: API returns empty user list

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is accessible but returns an empty list at /users.
test_setup.then: Logging shows start of fetch, zero users fetched, and does not attempt to log any user summaries. No errors are reported.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Verify that the application handles unexpected exceptions thrown by UserService gracefully.
test_cases.name: Internal error in UserService during fetch

test_setup.given: UserService.get_all_users() raises an exception (e.g., parsing error, unexpected response).
test_setup.then: An error is logged with details of the exception. No user summaries are logged.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Verify application behavior when the API returns malformed or invalid JSON.
test_cases.name: API returns invalid JSON

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint returns a non-JSON or malformed JSON response.
test_setup.then: An error is logged indicating a JSON parsing failure. No user summaries are logged.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Ensure that main() is not triggered when the script is imported as a module rather than run directly.
test_cases.name: Script imported as a module, main not executed

test_setup.given: src/main.py is imported from another module and not run as __main__.
test_setup.then: main() is not called. No logs related to user fetch or summaries are produced.

test_cases.description: Test how the application handles fetching and logging a very large user dataset.
test_cases.name: API returns a large number of users

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API returns a list of 1000+ user records at /users.
test_setup.then: Logging shows start, correct user count, and logs summaries for all users without error.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Check that the application does not crash and logs user summaries even if some user records have missing or null fields.
test_cases.name: API returns users with missing or null fields

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API returns user records with some fields missing or null (e.g., missing email or username).
test_setup.then: User summaries are logged; missing fields are handled gracefully without exceptions.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Verify that the application logs and handles HTTP error responses from /users endpoint.
test_cases.name: API returns HTTP error (e.g., 404, 500)

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint returns HTTP 500 error.
test_setup.then: An error is logged with the HTTP status code, and no user summaries are produced.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Verify application handles cases where /users returns a valid JSON object instead of a list.
test_cases.name: API returns valid JSON that is not a list

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint returns a valid JSON object, not a list.
test_setup.then: An error or warning is logged indicating unexpected response format. No user summaries are logged.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Verify the application logs an error when the /users request times out.
test_cases.name: API request timeout

test_setup.given: The request to JSONPlaceholder /users endpoint exceeds the timeout threshold.
test_setup.then: A timeout error is logged and no user summaries are processed.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Ensure the application ignores extra fields and logs expected user fields only.
test_cases.name: API returns additional unexpected fields

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API returns user records with additional non-standard fields.
test_setup.then: Application logs only the expected fields without error or transformation.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Verify that the application logs summaries even when fields have unexpected data types.
test_cases.name: User fields with incorrect data types

test_setup.given: API returns users where fields such as id, username, or email have incorrect data types.
test_setup.then: User summaries are logged without crashing, using the raw values.
test_setup.when: main() is executed as the entry point.

test_cases.description: Verify main() handles logging initialization errors.
test_cases.name: Logging initialization failure

test_setup.given: Logging initialization raises an exception (e.g., invalid file path).
test_setup.then: Application logs an initialization error and stops execution before fetching users.

test_cases.description: Verify the function returns the complete list of users when the JSONPlaceholder API is available and returns a valid response.
test_cases.name: Fetch all users successfully

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is reachable and returns a valid user list containing 10 user records.
test_setup.then: The function returns a list of 10 User domain model objects that exactly match the response from the API, with no filtering, sorting, or transformation.

test_cases.description: Ensure the function handles the case where the JSONPlaceholder API is unreachable, such as due to network errors or invalid endpoint.
test_cases.name: API unreachable error handling

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is unreachable (e.g., network failure, DNS error, or endpoint returns timeout).
test_setup.then: The function raises a connection-related exception or returns an error indicating the API cannot be reached and does not proceed with further processing.

test_cases.description: Verify the function handles non-200 HTTP status codes from the API gracefully.
test_cases.name: API returns HTTP error

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API returns an HTTP 500 Internal Server Error.
test_setup.then: The function raises an exception or returns an error indicating the API returned an error response, and does not process any user records.

test_cases.description: Check the function's handling when the API returns an empty user list.
test_cases.name: API returns empty user list

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is available and returns an empty list (no users).
test_setup.then: The function returns an empty list.

test_cases.description: Verify the function handles a malformed JSON response from the API.
test_cases.name: API returns malformed JSON

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is available but returns a malformed JSON response (e.g., incomplete object, invalid syntax).
test_setup.then: The function raises a JSON parsing error exception or returns an error indicating invalid/malformed JSON.

test_cases.description: Test the function's ability to handle large datasets efficiently and without errors.
test_cases.name: API returns a large list of users

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is available and returns a list of 10000 user records.
test_setup.then: The function returns a list of 10000 User domain model objects matching the API response.

test_cases.description: Check the function when the API returns exactly one user object in a list.
test_cases.name: API returns a single user

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is available and returns a list with a single user record.
test_setup.then: The function returns a list containing exactly one User domain model matching the API response.

test_cases.description: Ensure the function's handling of user records with missing fields from the API.
test_cases.name: API returns incomplete user data

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is available and returns a list of users where some user objects are missing required fields (e.g., 'id' or 'name').
test_setup.then: The function raises a data validation exception or returns user objects with missing/None fields, depending on implementation.

test_cases.description: Test the function if the API returns a valid JSON object instead of a list.
test_cases.name: API returns non-array JSON root

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is available but returns a JSON object (not a list/array) as the root response.
test_setup.then: The function raises a data type exception or returns an error indicating unexpected response format.

test_cases.description: Ensure the function does not filter, sort, or transform the user data received from the API.
test_cases.name: No modification of fetched user data

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API returns a user list with users in a specific order and with exact original field values.
test_setup.then: The function returns the list in the same order and with the same values as received from the API (i.e., no alteration).

test_cases.description: Verify UserService uses a provided UserRepository instance instead of creating a new one.
test_cases.name: Dependency injection with custom repository

test_setup.given: A mock UserRepository is injected into UserService and its fetch_all_users() returns a list of 3 mock users.
test_setup.then: The function returns exactly the list provided by the injected repository.

test_cases.description: Ensure unexpected repository-level errors propagate correctly.
test_cases.name: Repository fetch raises unexpected exception

test_setup.given: Injected UserRepository.fetch_all_users() raises an unexpected exception (e.g., RuntimeError).
test_setup.then: The exception is propagated upward without modification.

test_cases.description: Ensure function handles incorrect repository return type.
test_cases.name: Repository returns None instead of list

test_setup.given: Injected UserRepository.fetch_all_users() returns None.
test_setup.then: The function raises a type or validation exception indicating unexpected None response.

test_cases.description: Verify that UserService.__init__ prints 'test' as defined in pseudocode.
test_cases.name: Service initialization prints test

test_setup.given: UserService is instantiated.
test_setup.then: Output contains the string 'test'.

test_cases.description: Verify UserService creates a new UserRepository instance when none is injected.
test_cases.name: Default repository creation when none supplied

test_setup.given: UserService instantiated without parameters.
test_setup.then: Repository calls succeed using an internally created UserRepository instance.

test_cases.description: Verify that all user records are retrieved successfully when the JSONPlaceholder API is accessible and returns a valid response.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_success

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is reachable and /users endpoint responds with a valid user list.
test_setup.then: Returns a list of User domain models with all user records from the API.

test_cases.description: Ensure the function handles the scenario where the JSONPlaceholder API is unreachable (e.g., network failure, DNS failure, timeout) and reports the error.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_api_unreachable

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API is unreachable due to network error.
test_setup.then: Function raises an appropriate error or exception indicating the API is unreachable.

test_cases.description: Check that the function raises an error when the API responds with a non-2xx HTTP status code (e.g., 404, 500).
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_http_error

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint responds with HTTP 500 Internal Server Error.
test_setup.then: Function raises an HTTP error indicating the request failed.

test_cases.description: Verify that the function returns an empty list when the API returns an empty user list.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_empty_list

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint responds with an empty list [].
test_setup.then: Returns an empty list.

test_cases.description: Ensure that the function raises an error when the API returns invalid (non-JSON) response.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_invalid_json_response

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint responds with non-JSON or malformed JSON data.
test_setup.then: Function raises a JSON parse error.

test_cases.description: Ensure the function raises an error if a user record in the response cannot be parsed into the User domain model due to missing required fields.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_invalid_user_object

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint responds with a list containing a user object missing required fields.
test_setup.then: Function raises a parsing or validation error for the invalid user object.

test_cases.description: Validate that if the API returns a mix of valid and invalid user objects, the function fails with an error and does not return a partial list.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_partial_user_records

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint responds with a list containing valid and invalid user objects.
test_setup.then: Function raises an error rather than returning a partial list.

test_cases.description: Ensure that the function returns user records exactly as received from the API, with no filtering, sorting, or transformation.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_no_filtering_or_sorting

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint returns a list of user objects in a specific order.
test_setup.then: Returns user records in the same order and with the same data as the API response.

test_cases.description: Test the function's ability to handle large responses by simulating a large list of user objects.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_large_number_of_users

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint responds with a list of 1000 user objects.
test_setup.then: Returns a list of 1000 User domain models.

test_cases.description: Ensure the function handles slow API responses gracefully, either by waiting for the response or timing out as configured.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_slow_response

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users endpoint responds after a significant delay.
test_setup.then: Function either returns the expected list after waiting or raises a timeout error if one is configured.

test_cases.description: Verify URL is constructed using base_url and users_endpoint from Config.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_url_construction

test_setup.given: Valid Config values for base_url and users_endpoint.
test_setup.then: GET request is sent to base_url + users_endpoint exactly.

test_cases.description: Ensure timeout of 10 seconds is applied to the GET request.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_timeout_enforced

test_setup.given: API does not respond within 10 seconds.
test_setup.then: Function raises a timeout error.

test_cases.description: Ensure error is raised when API returns a JSON object instead of expected list.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_non_list_root

test_setup.given: API returns a JSON object instead of a list.
test_setup.then: Error is raised due to invalid response format.

test_cases.description: Ensure error is raised when API returns null.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_null_response

test_setup.given: API returns null as JSON.
test_setup.then: Function raises parsing or type error.

test_cases.description: Ensure extra unexpected fields in user object do not break parsing.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_extra_fields_ignored

test_setup.given: API returns valid user objects with additional fields.
test_setup.then: Parsing succeeds and extra fields are ignored.

test_cases.description: Ensure error is raised when API returns empty objects for users.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_empty_user_object

test_setup.given: API returns [{}].
test_setup.then: Function raises parsing error.

test_cases.description: Ensure repository respects overridden base_url and endpoint values.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_config_override

test_setup.given: Custom base_url and endpoint passed to UserRepository.
test_setup.then: GET request uses overridden values.

test_cases.description: Simulate connection reset during response.
test_cases.name: test_fetch_all_users_network_reset_midstream

test_setup.given: Connection drops mid-response.
test_setup.then: Function raises appropriate connection error.

test_cases.description: Verify that Config loads both environment variables when they are explicitly defined.
test_cases.name: load_config_with_all_env_vars_set

test_setup.given: Environment variables JSONPLACEHOLDER_BASE_URL='https://custom-api.com' and JSONPLACEHOLDER_USERS_ENDPOINT='/custom-users' are set.
test_setup.then: Config.JSONPLACEHOLDER_BASE_URL should equal 'https://custom-api.com' and Config.JSONPLACEHOLDER_USERS_ENDPOINT should equal '/custom-users'.

test_cases.description: Verify that Config falls back to default values when environment variables are missing.
test_cases.name: load_config_with_no_env_vars_set

test_setup.given: No environment variables are set.
test_setup.then: Config.JSONPLACEHOLDER_BASE_URL should default to 'https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com' and Config.JSONPLACEHOLDER_USERS_ENDPOINT should default to '/users'.

test_cases.description: Verify fallback to default endpoint when only JSONPLACEHOLDER_BASE_URL is set.
test_cases.name: load_config_with_only_base_url_set

test_setup.given: Environment variable JSONPLACEHOLDER_BASE_URL='https://custom-api.com' is set but JSONPLACEHOLDER_USERS_ENDPOINT is missing.
test_setup.then: Config.JSONPLACEHOLDER_BASE_URL should equal 'https://custom-api.com' and Config.JSONPLACEHOLDER_USERS_ENDPOINT should default to '/users'.

test_cases.description: Verify fallback to default base URL when only JSONPLACEHOLDER_USERS_ENDPOINT is set.
test_cases.name: load_config_with_only_users_endpoint_set

test_setup.given: Environment variable JSONPLACEHOLDER_USERS_ENDPOINT='/custom-users' is set but JSONPLACEHOLDER_BASE_URL is missing.
test_setup.then: Config.JSONPLACEHOLDER_BASE_URL should default to 'https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com' and Config.JSONPLACEHOLDER_USERS_ENDPOINT should equal '/custom-users'.

test_cases.description: Verify that .env file values override defaults.
test_cases.name: load_config_with_env_file_present

test_setup.given: .env file contains JSONPLACEHOLDER_BASE_URL='https://env-api.com' and JSONPLACEHOLDER_USERS_ENDPOINT='/env-users'.
test_setup.then: Config fields should reflect the .env values.
test_setup.when: The Config class loads environment variables.

test_cases.description: Verify behavior when .env file is absent.
test_cases.name: load_config_with_env_file_missing

test_setup.given: .env file does not exist and no environment variables are set.
test_setup.then: Config should load default values without raising errors.
test_setup.when: The Config class loads environment variables.

test_cases.description: Verify that empty strings are treated as set values, not defaults.
test_cases.name: load_config_with_empty_env_values

test_setup.given: Environment variables JSONPLACEHOLDER_BASE_URL='' and JSONPLACEHOLDER_USERS_ENDPOINT='' are set.
test_setup.then: Config values should be empty strings, not defaults.

test_cases.description: Verify behavior when the base URL contains invalid format.
test_cases.name: load_config_with_invalid_url_format

test_setup.given: Environment variable JSONPLACEHOLDER_BASE_URL='not-a-valid-url' is set.
test_setup.then: Config.JSONPLACEHOLDER_BASE_URL should equal 'not-a-valid-url' but downstream API calls would fail; Config should not validate or reject it.

test_cases.description: Verify endpoint behavior if missing expected format.
test_cases.name: load_config_with_missing_leading_slash_in_endpoint

test_setup.given: Environment variable JSONPLACEHOLDER_USERS_ENDPOINT='users' (no leading slash).
test_setup.then: Config.JSONPLACEHOLDER_USERS_ENDPOINT should equal 'users'. No automatic correction occurs.
test_setup.when: The Config class loads environment variables.

test_cases.description: System-level validation that correct Config values allow a proper request to JSONPlaceholder.
test_cases.name: fetch_users_with_valid_config

test_setup.given: Config uses default values and API is reachable.
test_setup.then: A GET request is sent to 'https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users' and full user list is returned.

test_cases.description: Verify application behavior when API is not reachable.
test_cases.name: fetch_users_with_unreachable_api

test_setup.given: Config values are valid but API domain cannot be reached.
test_setup.then: Application reports connection failure and does not proceed further.

test_cases.description: Verify system behavior when base URL and endpoint combine into malformed URL.
test_cases.name: fetch_users_with_malformed_combined_url

test_setup.given: JSONPLACEHOLDER_BASE_URL='https://api.com////' and JSONPLACEHOLDER_USERS_ENDPOINT='////users'.
test_setup.then: Final URL becomes malformed; API request fails and application must report failure.

test_cases.description: Validate behavior with boundary-length environment variables.
test_cases.name: fetch_users_with_extremely_long_env_values

test_setup.given: JSONPLACEHOLDER_BASE_URL is a very long string (e.g., 5000 characters).
test_setup.then: Config should accept the value without truncation, but API call may fail due to invalid URL.

test_cases.description: Parses a fully populated and valid user JSON dict into User, Address, and Company domain objects
test_cases.name: parse_user_happy_path_full_valid_data

test_setup.then: A User object is returned with all fields mapped exactly from the input

test_cases.description: Handles missing optional fields inside the address object if the domain model permits nulls
test_cases.name: parse_user_missing_optional_fields_in_address

test_setup.then: A User object is returned with missing fields represented as null where applicable

test_cases.description: Validates behavior when the entire address block is missing
test_cases.name: parse_user_missing_address_block

test_setup.then: Function raises a KeyError or equivalent exception due to missing address

test_cases.description: Validates behavior when the entire company block is missing
test_cases.name: parse_user_missing_company_block

test_setup.then: Function raises a KeyError or equivalent exception due to missing company

test_cases.description: Handles null geo values if domain model allows them
test_cases.name: parse_user_null_geo_coordinates

test_setup.then: User object is returned with geo fields set to null

test_cases.description: Fails when fields have invalid data types
test_cases.name: parse_user_invalid_data_types

test_setup.then: Function raises an exception due to invalid types

test_cases.description: Parses user object where fields are empty strings
test_cases.name: parse_user_empty_strings

test_setup.then: User object is returned with empty string fields preserved

test_cases.description: Handles extremely large numeric values for id
test_cases.name: parse_user_large_numeric_ids

test_setup.then: User object is returned with the large id preserved

test_cases.description: Tests behavior when geo object is present but missing lat or lng
test_cases.name: parse_user_nested_geo_missing_fields

test_setup.then: Function raises a KeyError due to missing lng field

test_cases.description: Tests behavior when all fields exist but are null
test_cases.name: parse_user_all_fields_null

test_setup.then: User object is returned with all null values
